operation: First, create a 2D sketch workplane on the XZ plane with origin at (0, 0, 0). Move the sketch cursor to the absolute coordinate (2, 4) on the workplane, draw a vertical line down to the point (2, 0). Next, draw a tangent arc ending at the absolute point (-2, 0). Then draw a vertical line up to (-2, 4), close the sketched profile to form a closed shape, and finally extrude the closed profile 6 units along the Y axis (the normal direction of the XZ workplane) to produce the final 3D solid.
geometry: This model is a uniform cross-section prism. Its cross-section is a closed shield-shaped profile: it has a straight horizontal top edge, two parallel vertical straight side edges extending downward from both ends of the top edge, and a smooth concave curved bottom edge connecting the bottom ends of the two vertical sides. The prism extends uniformly along the direction perpendicular to the cross-section plane, with flat parallel end faces at both ends of the extension direction.
function: This is a shield-shaped structural component, suitable for use as a small protective guard, decorative badge base, or standard insert part for assemblies that require a rounded lower edge and consistent cross-sectional thickness.
